activities.name: The Soft Snack Attack
activities.description: Learn to bring up a complaint without blame or criticism.
activities.detailedDescription: This level teaches you to replace "Blame" with "Soft Start-ups" to keep your heart rate calm and your partner's ears open.
steps: 1. The "I" Ingredient. Share your feeling first, like: "I feel excited when we try something new."
2. The Fact Finder. Say only what happened. Avoid blame words like "always" and "never."
3. The Positive Pitch. Ask clearly for what you want instead of only saying what is wrong.
4. The 24-Hour "Yes" Streak. Use this gentle tone for small requests today and notice the response.

activities.name: The Investigator
activities.description: Understand your partner's experience and perspective.
activities.detailedDescription: The goal isn't to agree; it's to understand. This level uses "Reflective Listening" to ensure both of you feel seen and respected.
steps: 1. The "Lead Witness" Statement. One partner shares a small frustration for 2 minutes. The other only listens.
2. The "Evidence Recap". Repeat what you heard in simple words to check if you understood.
3. The "Case Closed" Validation. Say: "That makes sense" before giving your own view.
4. The Role Reversal. Switch roles and repeat so both partners get a turn.

activities.name: The Great Compromise
activities.description: Shift from 'me vs you' to 'us vs the problem'.
activities.detailedDescription: This level moves you both from "Me vs. You" to "Us vs. The Problem" by finding common ground while protecting core needs.
steps: 1. The Treasure Chest. Write 3 needs that are very important for you to feel respected.
2. The Suitcase. List 3 things where you can adjust or try your partner's way.
3. The Island Charter. Create one solution that protects both partners' main needs.
4. The 7-Day Island Trial. Try the solution for one week, then talk about what worked.

activities.name: The Circuit Breaker
activities.description: Identify and break negative communication cycles.
activities.detailedDescription: This level helps in realizing that the "Argument Pattern" is the real enemy and teaches you how to call a healthy time-out.
steps: 1. Name the "Glitch". Give your usual fight pattern a funny name so it feels separate from both of you.
2. The Red Light Sensor. Say "Red Light" when your body feels heated or defensive.
3. The "Pause Menu" Ritual. Take a 20-minute break in separate spaces to cool down.
4. The Power-Up Hug. Come back, hug for 30 seconds, then restart the conversation.

activities.name: Love Map Update
activities.description: Update your knowledge of your partner's world.
activities.detailedDescription: Update your knowledge of your partner's world. This level turns you both into explorers rediscovering each other's current inner landscape.
steps: 1. The Curiosity Spark. Ask what hobby or interest they would start if there were no limits.
2. The "Boring" Detail Exchange. Share one small or funny detail from your day.
3. The Deep Dive Duo. When they share a story, ask: "How did that feel?"
4. The Big Reveal. Tell them one new thing you learned about them today.

activities.name: The Dopamine Drop
activities.description: Do something new to trigger first-date energy.
activities.detailedDescription: Do something new together to trigger first-date energy. This level uses novelty to release the same "honeymoon" chemicals found in early dating.
steps: 1. The Adventure Blueprint. List 5 fun things you both want to try this year.
2. The "Beginner" Challenge. Try one quick silly game or challenge together.
3. The 6-Second Connection. Put distractions away and share a 6-second kiss.
4. The Random Walk. Step outside and flip a coin to choose your next turn.

activities.name: Bid-Catching Pro
activities.description: Notice and respond to small connection bids.
activities.detailedDescription: Notice and respond to small connection bids from each other. This level trains both of you to be each other's biggest fans.
steps: 1. The "Caught You" Shout-out. Thank your partner for one specific thing they did recently.
2. The Origin Story Rewind. Share the moment you first felt this relationship was special.
3. The "Public" Brag. Tell or text someone one thing your partner is great at.
4. The Gratitude Triple-Play. Share 3 things you love about "us" right now.

activities.name: The Intimacy Architect
activities.description: Build a daily ritual that bonds you deeply.
activities.detailedDescription: Build a daily ritual that bonds you deeply. This level moves you from being individuals to a unified "Us."
steps: 1. The "Vault" Secret Code. Make a silly nickname or code word only you two use.
2. The Digital Blackout. Put phones away and sit together without distractions.
3. The Team Motto Manifesto. Pick 3 words that describe you at your best as a couple.
4. The Honeymoon Re-Up. Ask each other: "What made you feel closer today?"

activities.name: The Battery Check
activities.description: Check their energy before unloading your day.
activities.detailedDescription: Stop the "information dump" before it starts! This level turns checking in into a team strategy so you both feel heard without draining your "charge."
steps: 1. The Power Sync. Ask how much energy they have right now, from 1% to 100%.
2. The "Headlines" Only. Share 3 quick headlines from your day: high, low, and weird.
3. The Help Menu. Ask: "Do you want me to listen or help solve it?"
4. The Unified Recharge. Do one quick reset together, like a snack or short walk.

activities.name: No-Gatekeeping Needs
activities.description: Say what you need—no hints, no guessing.
activities.detailedDescription: Retire the guessing games. This level turns "expressing needs" into a collaborative map-making session where you both tell it like it is.
steps: 1. The "I" Statement Swap. Say what you feel, what happened, and what you need.
2. The Mind-Reader Resignation. Explain one thing you wish they knew without guessing.
3. The Secret Manual. Share one action that helps you feel loved or supported.
4. The "No-Hing" Pact. Pick a funny word to use when someone is hinting.

activities.name: The Echo Chamber
activities.description: Repeat to prove you're really listening.
activities.detailedDescription: Prove you are on the same team. This level gamifies "Reflective Listening" so that neither person feels ignored or misunderstood.
steps: 1. The 60-Second Solo. One person talks for 60 seconds while the other only listens.
2. The "Echo" Check. Repeat what you heard and ask: "Did I get that right?"
3. The Validation High-Five. If they feel misunderstood, try the summary again.
4. The Role Swap. Switch roles so both partners practice listening.

activities.name: Safe Space Protocol
activities.description: Create a no-judgment zone for honesty.
activities.detailedDescription: Build your "No-Judgment Zone." This level creates the rituals that keep your communication safe and honest for the long haul.
steps: 1. The "Red Light/Green Light" Ritual. Name triggers that make serious talks harder.
2. The Digital Blackout. Put phones away and sit face-to-face for a few minutes.
3. The "Reset" Signal. Create a phrase or gesture to pause heated talks.
4. The Communication Toast. Share one thing from this session that made you feel closer.
